title: Drinking water and health: Awareness resources - Canada.ca
content:
## Drinking water and health: Awareness resources
This page lists fact sheets and infographics for the general public about substances found in drinking water. These resources explain:
- the health effects of each substance
- ways to reduce your exposure
- any important information for sensitive groups such as infants, if applicable
If a substance isn’t listed here, more detailed information may be available under Technical resources . Health Canada has more than 100 technical guidelines and guidance documents on drinking water.
- Enteric protozoa ( Giardia and Cryptosporidium )
- Lead Drinking water: what about lead? (infographic)
- Drinking water: what about lead? (infographic)
- Legionella (infographic)
- Per-and polyfluoroalkyl substances (PFAS)
- Cleaning faucet aerators (infographic)
- Finding a drinking water filter (infographic)
- Water quality and health videos What the flood? Recreational water safety
- Recreational water safety
